title: Mellow Roasted Norwegian Fish Tagine
description: A pescatarian friendly Norwegian dish featuring fish sauce and nam pla, cooked in a traditional clay tagine vessel, resulting in a funky and tender meal prep option. This recipe is perfect for those looking for a unique and flavorful seafood experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the cod fillets and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a mortar and pestle, pound the garlic and ginger until well combined.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, nam pla, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pounded garlic and ginger to the bowl and stir to combine.
Place the cod fillets in the clay tagine vessel and brush the fish sauce mixture evenly over both fillets.
Cover the tagine with a lid and roast in the preheated oven for 12 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue roasting for an additional 8 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
